title: When Models Know When They Do Not Know: Calibration, Cascading, and Cleaning
abstract: The paper introduces a unified framework for model calibration that applies to both vision and language models across diverse tasks. Based on two core observations about confidence and calibration, the authors propose applications in model cascading and data cleaning.

core_observations: For a single model, confidence functions defined as likelihoods from logits exhibit an approximate monotonic relation
Confidence calibrated on a validation set remains calibrated on a held-out test set

applications.name: Model cascading
applications.description: A training-free cascading method using calibrated confidence to determine whether to invoke a larger model

applications.name: Data cleaning
applications.description: Using calibrated confidence with a mixture-of-experts approach for data cleaning

key_findings: Cascading achieves an effective balance of accuracy and efficiency
Cascading two state-of-the-art models achieves accuracy beyond the previous state of the art on ImageNet-1K
Data cleaning method outperforms prior methods in both accuracy and detection power

contributions: Formalizing a unified framework for calibration, cascading, and cleaning
Demonstrating the reliability and cross-model comparability of calibrated confidence
Showing effective balance of accuracy and efficiency through cascading
Outperforming prior methods in data cleaning
